briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. I will now begin the climate briefing for April 12, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Efforts for Carbon Neutrality Implementation and Awareness Raising
sections.content: Across South Korea, diverse activities are actively underway to implement carbon neutrality and enhance climate change awareness. The Gimhae Carbon Neutrality Experience Center and the Climate Change Theme Park hosted special events for Climate Change Week, and an international week-long event related to the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change was also held in Yeosu [1, 2]. Notably, Incheon City launched the 'Carbon Neutrality Youth Supporters' and Gwangmyeong City initiated the 'Youth Climate Academy' to strengthen the leadership of future generations. Jeju City, meanwhile, finalized the 'Top 5 Carbon Neutrality Action Tasks' directly selected by its citizens, emphasizing the importance of carbon reduction in daily life [8, 9, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16, 18, 19, 21, 23, 26, 27, 28]. These efforts are demonstrating tangible results in various sectors, including the introduction of smart water management systems [7], eco-friendly renewable energy agreements [20], the dissemination of electric motorcycles [22], the establishment of hydrogen transport complex bases [31], and Nonghyup's plan to expand its renewable energy share [52], showcasing the domestic society's commitment towards a sustainable future.

sections.title: ⚠️ Structural Constraints and Geopolitical Challenges in Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: Recent geopolitical instabilities, such as the Middle East situation, are amplifying concerns over energy security and further highlighting the necessity of transitioning to renewable energy [32, 36, 41, 44, 45, 47, 48, 51]. However, industry and experts point out that the pace of renewable energy transition is unlikely to accelerate immediately due to structural constraints [35, 39, 40, 42, 43, 46, 48]. Alongside the imperative for policy and institutional improvements to achieve a 20% renewable energy share [33], analyses also suggest that factors such as war and the surge in AI power consumption pose obstacles to this transition [34, 35]. Furthermore, warnings have been raised that rising oil prices could even impact renewable energy costs [49], indicating that energy transition is not a panacea. These complex factors underscore the need for in-depth discussions and strategic approaches to expand domestic renewable energy.

sections.title: 🌍 Global Awareness and Ecological Impacts of Climate Change
sections.content: Globally, climate change is already recognized as a present reality [55], with various studies demonstrating its severe impacts on ecosystems. Specifically, warnings that the pace of climate change is outstripping the evolutionary speed of species are repeatedly raised in various international news reports, and scientists are exploring solutions using DNA [59, 63, 64, 65, 66, 67, 68, 69, 70, 71, 72, 73]. These changes pose direct threats to specific industries and local livelihoods, such as the crisis facing New Hampshire's ski industry [56] and the demand for new approaches to protect Haor agriculture [61]. Furthermore, with Arctic seals facing the dual threat of climate change and toxic pollutants [62], and even research suggesting a link between increased underwater volcanic activity and climate change [74], it is evident that climate change is impacting the Earth's systems in unexpected ways.

sections.title: 🌱 Acceleration of Clean Energy Transition by the International Community
sections.content: Amidst these challenges, the international community continues to make positive strides towards clean energy transition. The UK recently set a new record in solar power generation, accelerating its clean energy transition [53], and is opening new possibilities in renewable energy storage through innovative technological development [54]. Furthermore, Canadian airports are also demonstrating concerted efforts to address climate change by exploring sustainable operational strategies [60], reflecting ongoing national endeavors. These international advancements reaffirm that technological development in renewable energy and policy support are crucial pillars in responding to the climate crisis.

briefing.closing: Today's collected climate-related news highlights ongoing efforts to combat climate change both domestically and internationally, while simultaneously revealing the structural limitations of renewable energy transition and the widespread ecological impacts of climate change. This concludes today's climate briefing.
